sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.6 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: (W.~Kimmerle). The prime graph (or Gruenberg--Kegel graph)$(X)$of a group$X$has vertices labeled by primes appearing as orders of elements in$X$; two distinct primes$p$and$q$are adjacent in$(X)$if and only if$X$contains an element of order$pq$. Denote by$( G)$the group of normalized units of the integral group ring of a group$G$. Is it true that for each finite group$G$the prime graphs of$G$and$( ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
